name: Nvidia Flow Emitter COMP
description: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.Hardware: This operator only works with Nvidia GPUs.
summary: OS: This operator is only supported under the Microsoft Windows operating system.Hardware: This operator only works with Nvidia GPUs.

parameters.name: Active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: Turns the emitter On or Off.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description:
mode -  - Select Emitter or Collider mode.

 emitter - Emitter will act as a fuel emitter for the system injecting a specific amount of fluw into the simulation each step. collider - Collider will act as a rigid object which the combustion simulation will collide with.

parameters.name: Emitter
parameters.label: Emitter
parameters.description: emitter - Emitter will act as a fuel emitter for the system injecting a specific amount of fluw into the simulation each step. collider - Collider will act as a rigid object which the combustion simulation will collide with.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description:
type -  - Select the shape type used for the emitter.

 sphere - Creates a sphere emitter, dimensions controlled by the Radius parameter below. box - Creates a box emitter, dimensions controlled by the Size parameter below. capsule - Create a capsule emitter, like a tube with cap ends. The dimensions are controlled by the Radius and Length parameters below. shapeop - Uses the  specified in the Shape  parameter below to create an emitter in the shape of the image. The Shape  cannot exceed 2048x2048 resolution. sop - Uses the  specified in the Shape OP parameter below to create an emitter with the shape of the 's geometry.

parameters.name: Sphere
parameters.label: Sphere
parameters.description: sphere - Creates a sphere emitter, dimensions controlled by the Radius parameter below. box - Creates a box emitter, dimensions controlled by the Size parameter below. capsule - Create a capsule emitter, like a tube with cap ends. The dimensions are controlled by the Radius and Length parameters below. shapeop - Uses the  specified in the Shape  parameter below to create an emitter in the shape of the image. The Shape  cannot exceed 2048x2048 resolution. sop - Uses the  specified in the Shape OP parameter below to create an emitter with the shape of the 's geometry.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.label: Size
parameters.description:
size -  - Controls the dimensions of the emitter when using Box or Shape  types.

 sizex - Scale in x. sizey - Scale in y. sizez - Scale in z.

parameters.name: X
parameters.label: X
parameters.description: sizex - Scale in x. sizey - Scale in y. sizez - Scale in z.

parameters.name: Radius
parameters.label: Radius
parameters.description: radius - Controls the radius of the emitter when using the Sphere or Capsule types.

parameters.name: Length
parameters.label: Length
parameters.description: length - Control the length of the emitter when using the Capsule type.

parameters.name: Shape OP
parameters.label: Shape OP
parameters.description: shapeop - Specify the  or  to use as an emitter when Type parameter is set to Shape  / Shape  respectively. For  emitters the resolution cannot exceed 2048x2048.

parameters.name: Shape Channel
parameters.label: Shape Channel
parameters.description: shapechannel - Allows use of different channels to determine the shape of the emitter created when using Shape .

parameters.name: Shape Threshold
parameters.label: Shape Threshold
parameters.description: shapethreshold - Pixels greater than or equal to the threshold value are used for emission, pixels below the threshold value are ignored.

parameters.name: Center of Mass
parameters.label: Center of Mass
parameters.description:
centerofmass -  - Specifies the center of mass of the emitter.

 centerofmassx - centerofmassy - centerofmassz -

parameters.name: Inner Width
parameters.label: Inner Width
parameters.description: innerwidth - The shape of the emitter sets the surface shape, and the Inner Width adds to the emitter by filling in the shape from the surface inwards. The default setting of 1.0 effectively fills in the shape like a solid.

parameters.name: Outer Width
parameters.label: Outer Width
parameters.description: outerwidth - The shape of the emitter set the surface shape, and the Outer Width adds to the emitter by extending the shape outwards from the surface.

parameters.name: Linear Velocity
parameters.label: Linear Velocity
parameters.description:
linearvel -  - Target linear velocity of the fuel added to the system.

 linearvelx - Linear velocity in x. linearvely - Linear velocity in y. linearvelz - Linear velocity in z.

parameters.name: Angular Velocity
parameters.label: Angular Velocity
parameters.description:
angularvel -  - Target angular velocity of the fuel added to the system. Think of this as 'rotational velocity.

 angularvelx - Angular velocity in x. angularvely - Angular velocity in y. angularvelz - Angular velocity in z.

parameters.name: Velocity Correction Rate
parameters.label: Velocity Correction Rate
parameters.description:
velcorrate -  - Rate at which the the system gets to the target velocity. Each simulation block has its own velocity level, the emitter tries to change any blocks it overlaps with to match its emitter value. The correction rate is how strongly it tries to change the value to this target value, for example if 0 the emiitter won't do anything, when a small value like 0-1 the emitter will gently influence the simulation, when a high value like 10-100 it will force the value.

 velcorratex - velcorratey - velcorratez -

parameters.name: Smoke
parameters.label: Smoke
parameters.description: smoke - Amount of smoke produced per unit of fuel in the system.

parameters.name: Smoke Correction Rate
parameters.label: Smoke Correction Rate
parameters.description: smokecorrate - Rate at which the the system gets to the target smoke level. Each simulation block has its own smoke level, the emitter tries to change any blocks it overlaps with to match its emitter value. The correction rate is how strongly it tries to change the value to this target value, for example if 0 the emiitter won't do anything, when a small value like 0-1 the emitter will gently influence the simulation, when a high value like 10-100 it will force the value.

parameters.name: Temp
parameters.label: Temp
parameters.description: temp - Temperature of the system. Note a temperature value of 0 will not ignite the simlulation, a minimum temperature is required for the simulation to burn.

parameters.name: Temp Correction Rate
parameters.label: Temp Correction Rate
parameters.description: tempcorrate - Rate at which the the system gets to the target temperature level. Each simulation block has its own temperature level, the emitter tries to change any blocks it overlaps with to match its emitter value. The correction rate is how strongly it tries to change the value to this target value, for example if 0 the emiitter won't do anything, when a small value like 0-1 the emitter will gently influence the simulation, when a high value like 10-100 it will force the value.

parameters.name: Fuel
parameters.label: Fuel
parameters.description: fuel - Amount of fuel added to the system per simulation step. The fuel is treated as gaseous and is converted into temperature (and a certain density) when combustion is taking place.

parameters.name: Fuel Correction Rate
parameters.label: Fuel Correction Rate
parameters.description: fuelcorrate - Rate at which the the system gets to the target fuel level. Each simulation block has its own fuel level, the emitter tries to change any blocks it overlaps with to match its emitter value. The correction rate is how strongly it tries to change the value to this target value, for example if 0 the emiitter won't do anything, when a small value like 0-1 the emitter will gently influence the simulation, when a high value like 10-100 it will force the value.

parameters.name: Fuel Release Temp
parameters.label: Fuel Release Temp
parameters.description: fuelreleasetemp - THe temperature required in the system to trigger addition Fuel Release set in the parameter below.

parameters.name: Fuel Release
parameters.label: Fuel Release
parameters.description: fuelrelease - An additional amount of fuel is released into the system when the Fuel Release Temperature threshold set above is met. TIP: This can help simulate solid fuels where heat must be applied for a gaseous fuel to be released.

parameters.name: Alloc Scale
parameters.label: Alloc Scale
parameters.description:
allocscale - The emitter allocates 'blocks' to contain the simulation. This parameter controls this where 0.0 turns off emitter allocation, 1.0 is default, and values greater than 1.0 can help pre-allocate which can be useful when the direction of the simulation is difficult to predict.
The effect of this parameter can be visualized by turning 'Show Blocks' parameter On in the Nvidia Flow TOP.

parameters.name: Alloc Predict
parameters.label: Alloc Predict
parameters.description:
allocpredict - Controls the emitter's direction block allocation prediction, used for pre-allocation of blocks for fast emitters.
The effect of this parameter can be visualized by turning 'Show Blocks' parameter On in the Nvidia Flow TOP.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
color -  - The base color of the combustion.

 colorr - colorg - colorb - colora -

parameters.name: Red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: colorr - colorg - colorb - colora -

parameters.name: Color Ramp
parameters.label: Color Ramp
parameters.description: colorramp - This color ramp will be multiplied with the Color Parameter above. The left-side of the color ramp is used for the cooler temperatures in the system ie. further away from center of combustion and where it turns to smoke. The right-side of the ramp colors the higher temperatures.

parameters.name: Alpha Scale
parameters.label: Alpha Scale
parameters.description: alphascale - Alpha calculations (determined by Alpha Bias and Alpha Masks below) are multiplied by this value for a final Alpha value.

parameters.name: Alpha Bias
parameters.label: Alpha Bias
parameters.description: alphabias - This value is added to alpha before final alpha values are calculated using the Alpha Mask parameters below.

parameters.name: Intensity Bias
parameters.label: Intensity Bias
parameters.description: intensitybias - A mulitplier for the color contribution. Useful to use with the Additive Factor parameter below.

parameters.name: Additive Factor
parameters.label: Additive Factor
parameters.description: additivefactor - Controls an additive effect in the rendering ie. transparency is added together giving hotspots and brighter color where there is more gas burning.

parameters.name: Burn Color Mask
parameters.label: Burn Color Mask
parameters.description: burncolormask - Controls the color contribution of the ignition point of the fuel in the system. The value determines which position in the Color Ramp to use for Burn color.

parameters.name: Smoke Color Mask
parameters.label: Smoke Color Mask
parameters.description: smokecolormask - Controls the color contribution of the smoke in the system. The value determines which position in the Color Ramp to use for Smoke color.

parameters.name: Temp Color Mask
parameters.label: Temp Color Mask
parameters.description: tempcolormask - Controls the color contribution of the temperature in the system. Note: Fuel is converted to Temperature when combustion is active. The value determines which position in the Color Ramp to use for Temp color.

parameters.name: Fuel Color Mask
parameters.label: Fuel Color Mask
parameters.description: fuelcolormask - Controls the color contribution of the fuel in the system before it is burned. The value determines which position in the Color Ramp to use for Fuel color.

parameters.name: Burn Alpha Mask
parameters.label: Burn Alpha Mask
parameters.description: burnalphamask - Controls the transparency of the Burn Color above.

parameters.name: Smoke Alpha Mask
parameters.label: Smoke Alpha Mask
parameters.description: smokealphamask - Controls the transparency of the Smoke Color above.

parameters.name: Temp Alpha Mask
parameters.label: Temp Alpha Mask
parameters.description: tempalphamask - Controls the transparency of the Temp Color above.

parameters.name: Fuel Alpha Mask
parameters.label: Fuel Alpha Mask
parameters.description: fuelalphamask - Controls the transparency of the Fuel Color above.

parameters.name: Burn Intensity Mask
parameters.label: Burn Intensity Mask
parameters.description: burnintensitymask - Controls the intensity of the Burn Color above. This value is a multiplier and accepts values greater than 1.0.

parameters.name: Smoke Intensity Mask
parameters.label: Smoke Intensity Mask
parameters.description: smokeintensitymask - Controls the intensity of the Smoke Color above. This value is a multiplier and accepts values greater than 1.0.

parameters.name: Temp Intensity Mask
parameters.label: Temp Intensity Mask
parameters.description: tempintensitymask - Controls the intensity of the Temp Color above. This value is a multiplier and accepts values greater than 1.0.

parameters.name: Fuel Intensity Mask
parameters.label: Fuel Intensity Mask
parameters.description: fuelintensitymask - Controls the intensity of the Fuel Color above. This value is a multiplier and accepts values greater than 1.0.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
xord -  - This allows you to specify the order in which the changes to your  will take place. Changing the Transform Order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you to a different place than turning east and then going a block. In matrix math terms, if we use the 'multiply vector on the right' (column vector) convention, a transform order of Scale, Rotate, Translate would be written as T * R * S * Position.

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.label: Scale Rotate Translate
parameters.description: srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
rord -  - This allows you to set the transform order for the 's rotations. As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the 's rotations take place will alter the 's final position.	A Rotation order of Rx Ry Rz would create the final rotation matrix as follows R = Rz * Ry * Rx

 xyz - R = Rz * Ry * Rx xzy - R = Ry * Rz * Rx yxz - R = Rz * Rx * Ry yzx - R = Rx * Rz * Ry zxy - R = Ry * Rx * Rz zyx - R = Rx * Ry * Rz

parameters.name: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.label: Rx Ry Rz
parameters.description: xyz - R = Rz * Ry * Rx xzy - R = Ry * Rz * Rx yxz - R = Rz * Rx * Ry yzx - R = Rx * Rz * Ry zxy - R = Ry * Rx * Rz zyx - R = Rx * Ry * Rz

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description:
t -  - This allows you to specify the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an alternative to entering the values directly into these fields, you can modify the values by manipulating the  in the Viewport with the Select & Transform state.

 tx - ty - tz -

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description:
r -  - Theis specifies the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an alternative to entering the values directly into these fields, you can modify the values by manipulating the  in the Viewport with the Select & Transform state.

 rx - ry - rz -

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description:
s -  - This specifies the amount of movement along any of the three axes; the amount, in degrees, of rotation around any of the three axes; and a non-uniform scaling along the three axes. As an alternative to entering the values directly into these fields, you can modify the values by manipulating the  in the Viewport with the Select & Transform state.

 sx - sy - sz -

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description:
p -  - The Pivot point edit fields allow you to define the point about which a  scales and rotates. Altering the pivot point of a  produces different results depending on the transformation performed on the .	
For example, during a scaling operation, if the pivot point of an  is located at -1, -1, 0 and you wanted to scale the  by 0.5 (reduce its size by 50%), the  would scale toward the pivot point and appear to slide down and to the left.			
			
In the example above, rotations performed on an  with different pivot points produce very different results.


 px - py - pz -

parameters.name: Uniform Scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description:
scale - This field allows you to change the size of an  uniformly along the three axes.	
 Scaling a camera's channels is not generally recommended. However, should you decide to do so, the rendered output will match the Viewport as closely as possible when scales are involved.

parameters.name: Parent Transform Source
parameters.label: Parent Transform Source
parameters.description:
parentxformsrc -  - Select what position is used as the transform source for this obejct. Can be one of "Parent ()", "Specify Parent ", or "World Origin".

 hierarchy - specify - worldorigin -

parameters.name: From Parent Object (Hierarchy)
parameters.label: From Parent Object (Hierarchy)
parameters.description: hierarchy - specify - worldorigin -

parameters.name: Parent Object
parameters.label: Parent Object
parameters.description: parentobject - Allows the location of the object to be constrained to any other object whose path is specified in this parameter.

parameters.name: Look At
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description:
lookat - Allows you to orient this  by naming another 3D  you would like it to , or point to. Once you have designated this  to look at, it will continue to face that , even if you move it. This is useful if, for instance, you want a camera to follow another 's movements. The  parameter points the  in question at the other 's origin.	
 To designate a center of interest for the camera that doesn't appear in your scene, create a Null  and disable its display flag. Then Parent the Camera to the newly created Null , and tell the camera to look at this  using the  parameter. You can direct the attention of the camera by moving the Null  with the Select state. If you want to see both the camera and the Null , enable the Null 's display flag, and use the Select state in an additional Viewport by clicking one of the icons in the top-right corner of the TouchDesigner window.

parameters.name: Forward Direction
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description:
forwarddir -  - Sets which axis and direction is considered the forward direction.

 posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: +X
parameters.label: +X
parameters.description: posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: Look At Up Vector
parameters.label: Look At Up Vector
parameters.description:
lookup -  - When specifying a , it is possible to specify an up vector for the lookat. Without using an up vector, it is possible to get poor animation when the lookat , for example, passes through the Y axis of the target .	
 - Use this option if the look at  does not pass through the Y axis of the target .
 - This precisely defines the rotates on the  doing the looking. The  specified should not be parallel to the look at direction. See  below.
 - Quaternions are a mathematical representation of a 3D rotation. This method finds the most efficient means of moving from one point to another on a sphere.
 off - on - quat - roll -

parameters.name: Don't use up vector
parameters.label: Don't use up vector
parameters.description: off - on - quat - roll -

parameters.name: Path SOP
parameters.label: Path SOP
parameters.description: pathsop - Names the  that functions as the path you want this  to move along. For instance, you can name a  that provides a path for the camera to follow.

parameters.name: Roll
parameters.label: Roll
parameters.description: roll - Using the angle control you can specify a 's rotation as it animates along the path.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.label: Position
parameters.description: pos - This parameter lets you specify the  of the  along the path. The values you can enter for this parameter range from 0 to 1, where 0 equals the starting point and 1 equals the end point of the path. The value slider allows for values as high as 10 for multiple "passes" along the path.

parameters.name: Orient along Path
parameters.label: Orient along Path
parameters.description: pathorient - If this option is selected, the  will be oriented along the path. The positive Z axis of the  will be pointing down the path.

parameters.name: Orient Up Vector
parameters.label: Orient Up Vector
parameters.description:
up -  - When orienting a , the  is used to determine where the positive Y axis points.

 upx - upy - upz -

parameters.name: Auto-Bank Factor
parameters.label: Auto-Bank Factor
parameters.description: bank - The  rolls the  based on the curvature of the path at its current position. To turn off auto-banking, set the bank scale to 0.

parameters.name: Apply Pre-Transform
parameters.label: Apply Pre-Transform
parameters.description: pxform - Enables the transformation on this page.

parameters.name: Reset Transform
parameters.label: Reset Transform
parameters.description: preset - This button will reset this page's transform so it has no translate/rotate/scale.

parameters.name: Commit to Main Transform
parameters.label: Commit to Main Transform
parameters.description: pcommit - This button will copy the transform from this page to the main Xform page, and reset this page's transform.

parameters.name: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.label: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: xformmatrixop - This parameter can be used to transform using a 4x4 matrix directly. For information on ways to specify a matrix directly, refer to the Matrix Parameters page. This transform will be applied after the regular Pre-Transform transformation. That is, it'll be applied in the oder XformMatrix * PreXForm * Position.

parameters.name: Instancing
parameters.label: Instancing
parameters.description: instancing - Turns on instancing for the Geometry .

parameters.name: Instance Count Mode
parameters.label: Instance Count Mode
parameters.description:
instancecountmode -  - Two modes to determine how many instances will be created.

 manual - Use the Num Instances parameter below to set the number of instances. oplength - The number of  samples/ rows in the  / determines the number of instances.

parameters.name: Manual
parameters.label: Manual
parameters.description: manual - Use the Num Instances parameter below to set the number of instances. oplength - The number of  samples/ rows in the  / determines the number of instances.

parameters.name: Num Instances
parameters.label: Num Instances
parameters.description: numinstances - When using the Manual mode for  Count, this parameter set the number of instances.

parameters.name: Default Instance OP
parameters.label: Default Instance OP
parameters.description: instanceop - Specify a path to a  or  used to transform the instances. Number of samples/rows in this  or  determines the number of instances when using the  Length/ Num Rows mode for  Count.

parameters.name: First Row is
parameters.label: First Row is
parameters.description:
instancefirstrow -  - What to do with the first row of a table  when using  rows for  Count.

 ignored - The first row is ignored and it's values won't be used as part of an instance. Indices must be used to select the columns to use for instance attributes. names - The first row contains column names which can be used to select which columns to use from the table. values - The first row is considered to contain values for the first instance. Indices must be used to select the columns to use for instance attributes.

parameters.name: Ignored
parameters.label: Ignored
parameters.description: ignored - The first row is ignored and it's values won't be used as part of an instance. Indices must be used to select the columns to use for instance attributes. names - The first row contains column names which can be used to select which columns to use from the table. values - The first row is considered to contain values for the first instance. Indices must be used to select the columns to use for instance attributes.

parameters.name: Translate OP
parameters.label: Translate OP
parameters.description: instancetop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Translate instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Translate X
parameters.label: Translate X
parameters.description: instancetx - Select what data to use to translate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Translate Y
parameters.label: Translate Y
parameters.description: instancety - Select what data to use to translate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Translate Z
parameters.label: Translate Z
parameters.description: instancetz - Select what data to use to translate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate OP
parameters.label: Rotate OP
parameters.description: instancerop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Rotate instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Rotate X
parameters.label: Rotate X
parameters.description: instancerx - Select what data to use to rotate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate Y
parameters.label: Rotate Y
parameters.description: instancery - Select what data to use to rotate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate Z
parameters.label: Rotate Z
parameters.description: instancerz - Select what data to use to rotate instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Scale OP
parameters.label: Scale OP
parameters.description: instancesop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Scale instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Scale X
parameters.label: Scale X
parameters.description: instancesx - Select what data to use to scale instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Scale Y
parameters.label: Scale Y
parameters.description: instancesy - Select what data to use to scale instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Scale Z
parameters.label: Scale Z
parameters.description: instancesz - Select what data to use to scale instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Pivot OP
parameters.label: Pivot OP
parameters.description: instancepop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Pivot instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Pivot X
parameters.label: Pivot X
parameters.description: instancepx - Select what data to use for the pivot of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Pivot Y
parameters.label: Pivot Y
parameters.description: instancepy - Select what data to use for the pivot of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Pivot Z
parameters.label: Pivot Z
parameters.description: instancepz - Select what data to use for the pivot of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate to Vector: Order
parameters.label: Rotate to Vector: Order
parameters.description:
instancerottoorder -  - Controls where in the transform equation the Rotate To Vector operation is applied.

 default - The Rotate to Vector operation will be applied before all other transform operations (except the pivot offset), regardless of their order of operation. E.g  T * R * S * (RotToVector) * Position ,  R * S * T * (RotToVector) * Position . prerot - The Rotate To Vector operation will be applied after the main rotation as part of the TRS order. I.e  T * (RotToVector * R) * S * Position,  (RotToVector * R) * S * T * Position. postrot - The Rotate To Vector operation will be applied before the main rotation as part of the TRS order. I.e  T * (R * RotToVector) * S * Position,  (R * RotToVector) * S * T * Position.

parameters.name: Default
parameters.label: Default
parameters.description: default - The Rotate to Vector operation will be applied before all other transform operations (except the pivot offset), regardless of their order of operation. E.g  T * R * S * (RotToVector) * Position ,  R * S * T * (RotToVector) * Position . prerot - The Rotate To Vector operation will be applied after the main rotation as part of the TRS order. I.e  T * (RotToVector * R) * S * Position,  (RotToVector * R) * S * T * Position. postrot - The Rotate To Vector operation will be applied before the main rotation as part of the TRS order. I.e  T * (R * RotToVector) * S * Position,  (R * RotToVector) * S * T * Position.

parameters.name: Rotate to Vector: Forward Direction
parameters.label: Rotate to Vector: Forward Direction
parameters.description:
instancerottoforward -  - Determine which axis for the geometry original orientation is considered 'forward'. That is, it'll treat the part of the geometry that is looking down that axis as the front and rotate it so it's aligned with the rotate to vector direction.

 posx - negx - posy - negy - posz - negz -

parameters.name: Rotate to OP
parameters.label: Rotate to OP
parameters.description: instancerottoop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Rotate to Vector instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Rotate to Vector X
parameters.label: Rotate to Vector X
parameters.description: instancerottox - Select what data to use to rotate to vector instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate to Vector Y
parameters.label: Rotate to Vector Y
parameters.description: instancerottoy - Select what data to use to rotate to vector instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate to Vector Z
parameters.label: Rotate to Vector Z
parameters.description: instancerottoz - Select what data to use to rotate to vector instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate Up OP
parameters.label: Rotate Up OP
parameters.description: instancerotupop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Rotate Up instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Rotate Up X
parameters.label: Rotate Up X
parameters.description: instancerotupx - Select what data to use to rotate up instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Rotate Up Y
parameters.label: Rotate Up Y
parameters.description: instancerotupy - Select what data to use to rotate up instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options

parameters.name: Rotate Up Z
parameters.label: Rotate Up Z
parameters.description: instancerotupz - Select what data to use to rotate up instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options

parameters.name: Instance Order
parameters.label: Instance Order
parameters.description:
instanceorder -  - Sets how transforms are applied to the instances.

 instanceworld - Use the individual instance transforms first, then apply the world transform (i.e. Xform and Pre-Xform parameter pages). worldXform * instanceXForm * Position worldinstance - Use the world transform first, then apply the individual instance transforms. instanceXForm * worldXForm * Position

parameters.name: Instance, then World Transform
parameters.label: Instance, then World Transform
parameters.description: instanceworld - Use the individual instance transforms first, then apply the world transform (i.e. Xform and Pre-Xform parameter pages). worldXform * instanceXForm * Position worldinstance - Use the world transform first, then apply the individual instance transforms. instanceXForm * worldXForm * Position

parameters.name: Texture Mode
parameters.label: Texture Mode
parameters.description:
instancetexmode -  - Set how the texture coordinates are applied to the instances.

 replace - Replaces texture coordinates. transform - Offsets texture coordinates.

parameters.name: Replace
parameters.label: Replace
parameters.description: replace - Replaces texture coordinates. transform - Offsets texture coordinates.

parameters.name: Tex Coord OP
parameters.label: Tex Coord OP
parameters.description: instancetexcoordop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Texture Coord instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: U
parameters.label: U
parameters.description: instanceu - Select what data to apply to texture coordinates of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. This interacts with the first texture layer uv[0] attributes coming from the .

parameters.name: V
parameters.label: V
parameters.description: instancev - Select what data to apply to texture coordinates of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. This interacts with the first texture layer uv[0] attributes coming from the .

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: instancew - Select what data to apply to texture coordinates of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. This interacts with the first texture layer uv[0] attributes coming from the .

parameters.name: Color Mode
parameters.label: Color Mode
parameters.description:
instancecolormode -  - Controls how the instance color values interact with the SOPs 'Cd' (diffuse color) attribute. If the  doesn't have a 'Cd' attribute, then it will behave as if its 'Cd' is (1, 1, 1, 1).

 replace - multiply - add - subtract -

parameters.name: Color OP
parameters.label: Color OP
parameters.description: instancecolorop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Color instance attributes below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: R
parameters.label: R
parameters.description: instancer - Select what data to apply to the diffuse color of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. These parameters will be combined/replaced with the SOPs 'Cd' attribute, as chosen by the Color Mode parameter.

parameters.name: G
parameters.label: G
parameters.description: instanceg - Select what data to apply to the diffuse color of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. These parameters will be combined/replaced with the SOPs 'Cd' attribute, as chosen by the Color Mode parameter.

parameters.name: B
parameters.label: B
parameters.description: instanceb - Select what data to apply to the diffuse color of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. These parameters will be combined/replaced with the SOPs 'Cd' attribute, as chosen by the Color Mode parameter.

parameters.name: A
parameters.label: A
parameters.description: instancea - Select what data to apply to the diffuse color of the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options. These parameters will be combined/replaced with the SOPs 'Cd' attribute, as chosen by the Color Mode parameter.

parameters.name: Instance Textures
parameters.label: Instance Textures
parameters.description:
instancetexs -  - Specify the paths one or more  containing the textures to use with the instances. Wildcards and pattern matching is supported.

  instancetexextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  instancetexanisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: Extend U
parameters.label: Extend U
parameters.description:
instancetexextendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  instancetexfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  instancetexanisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.label: Hold
parameters.description: hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: Extend V
parameters.label: Extend V
parameters.description:
instancetexextendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: Extend W
parameters.label: Extend W
parameters.description:
instancetexextendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.label: Filter
parameters.description:
instancetexfilter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

parameters.name: Nearest
parameters.label: Nearest
parameters.description: nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

parameters.name: Anisotropic Filter
parameters.label: Anisotropic Filter
parameters.description:
instancetexanisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: Tex Index OP
parameters.label: Tex Index OP
parameters.description: instancetexindexop - Select a specific operator to get data from for the Texture Index instance attribute below. If not specified, the the operator specified in the 'Default  ' on the  parameter page can be used.

parameters.name: Texture Index
parameters.label: Texture Index
parameters.description: instancetexindex - Select what data to select which texture to use for the instances, use the drop-down menu on the right to easily select from the available options.

parameters.name: Material
parameters.label: Material
parameters.description: material - Selects a MAT to apply to the geometry inside.

parameters.name: Render
parameters.label: Render
parameters.description: render - Whether the 's geometry is visible in the Render TOP. This parameter works in conjunction (logical AND) with the 's Render Flag.

parameters.name: Draw Priority
parameters.label: Draw Priority
parameters.description: drawpriority - Determines the order in which the Components are drawn. Smaller values get drawn after larger values. The value is compared with other Components in the same parent , or if the  is the top level one listed in the Render 's '' parameter, then against other top-level Components listed there. This value is most often used to help with Transparency.

parameters.name: Pick Priority
parameters.label: Pick Priority
parameters.description: pickpriority - When using a Render Pick CHOP or a Render Pick DAT, there is an option to have a 'Search Area'. If multiple objects are found within the search area, the pick priority can be used to select one object over another. A higher value will get picked over a lower value. This does not affect draw order, or objects that are drawn over each other on the same pixel. Only one will be visible for a pick per pixel.

parameters.name: Wireframe Color
parameters.label: Wireframe Color
parameters.description:
wcolor -  - Use the R, G, and B fields to set the 's color when displayed in wireframe shading mode.

 wcolorr - wcolorg - wcolorb -

parameters.name: Light Mask
parameters.label: Light Mask
parameters.description: lightmask - By default all lights used in the Render TOP will affect geometry renderer. This parameter can be used to specify a sub-set of lights to be used for this particular geometry. The lights must be listed in the Render TOP as well as this parameter to be used.

parameters.name: Extension
parameters.label: Extension
parameters.description:
ext - Sequence of info for creating extensions on this component



  ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.


    
  ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.



  ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Object
parameters.label: Object
parameters.description: ext0object - A number of class instances that can be attached to the component.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: ext0name - Optional name to search by, instead of the instance class name.

parameters.name: Promote
parameters.label: Promote
parameters.description: ext0promote - Controls whether or not the extensions are visible directly at the component level, or must be accessed through the .ext member.  Example:  n.Somefunction vs n.ext.Somefunction

parameters.name: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.label: Re-Init Extensions
parameters.description: reinitextensions - Recompile all extension objects. Normally extension objects are compiled only when they are referenced and their definitions have changed.

parameters.name: Parent Shortcut
parameters.label: Parent Shortcut
parameters.description: parentshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as the path to that component. See Parent Shortcut.

parameters.name: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.label: Global OP Shortcut
parameters.description: opshortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere at all as the path to that component. See Global OP Shortcut.

parameters.name: Internal OP
parameters.label: Internal OP
parameters.description:
iop - Sequence header for internal operators.



  iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.



  iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Shortcut
parameters.label: Shortcut
parameters.description: iop0shortcut - Specifies a name you can use anywhere inside the component as a path to "Internal " below. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: OP
parameters.label: OP
parameters.description: iop0op - The path to the Internal OP inside this component. See Internal Operators.

parameters.name: Node View
parameters.label: Node View
parameters.description:
nodeview -  - Determines what is displayed in the node viewer, also known as the Node Viewer. Some options will not be available depending on the  type (Object Component, Panel Component, Misc.)

 default - Displays the default viewer for the component type, a 3D Viewer for Object COMPS and a  Viewer for Panel COMPs. opviewer - Displays the node viewer from any operator specified in the Operator Viewer parameter below.

parameters.name: Default Viewer
parameters.label: Default Viewer
parameters.description: default - Displays the default viewer for the component type, a 3D Viewer for Object COMPS and a  Viewer for Panel COMPs. opviewer - Displays the node viewer from any operator specified in the Operator Viewer parameter below.

parameters.name: Operator Viewer
parameters.label: Operator Viewer
parameters.description: opviewer - Select which operator's node viewer to use when the Node View parameter above is set to Operator .

parameters.name: Enable Cloning
parameters.label: Enable Cloning
parameters.description: enablecloning - Control if the OP should be actively cloneing. Turning this off causes this node to stop cloning it's ' Master'.

parameters.name: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.label: Enable Cloning Pulse
parameters.description: enablecloningpulse - Instantaneously clone the contents.

parameters.name: Clone Master
parameters.label: Clone Master
parameters.description: clone -  to a component used as the Master Clone.

parameters.name: Load on Demand
parameters.label: Load on Demand
parameters.description: loadondemand - Loads the component into memory only when required. Good to use for components that are not always used in the project.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox
parameters.label: Enable External .tox
parameters.description: enableexternaltox - When on (default), the external  file will be loaded when the  starts and the contents of the  will match that of the external . This can be turned off to avoid loading from the referenced external  on startup if desired (the contents of the  are instead loaded from the  file). Useful if you wish to have a  reference an external  but not always load from it unless you specifically push the Re-Init Network parameter button.

parameters.name: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.label: Enable External .tox Pulse
parameters.description: enableexternaltoxpulse - This button will re-load from the external  file (if present).

parameters.name: External .tox Path
parameters.label: External .tox Path
parameters.description: externaltox -  to a  file on disk which will source the component's contents upon start of a . This allows for components to contain networks that can be updated independently. If the  file can not be found, whatever the  file was saved with will be loaded.

parameters.name: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Custom Parameters
parameters.description: reloadcustom - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's Custom Parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.label: Reload Built-In Parameters
parameters.description: reloadbuiltin - When this checkbox is enabled, the values of the component's built-in parameters are reloaded when the .tox is reloaded. This only affects top-level parameters on the component, all parameters on nodes inside the component are always reloaded with the .tox.

parameters.name: Save Backup of External
parameters.label: Save Backup of External
parameters.description: savebackup - When this checkbox is enabled, a backup copy of the component specified by the External  parameter is saved in the  file.  This backup copy will be used if the External  can not be found. This may happen if the  was renamed, deleted, or the  file is running on another computer that is missing component media.

parameters.name: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.label: Sub-Component to Load
parameters.description: subcompname - When loading from an External  file, this option allows you to reach into the  and pull out a  and make that the top-level , ignoring everything else in the file (except for the contents of that ). For example if a  file named project1 contains project1/geo1, putting geo1 as the Sub- to Load, will result in geo1 being loaded in place of the current . If this parameter is blank, it just loads the  file normally using the top level  in the file.

parameters.name: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.label: Relative File Path Behavior
parameters.description:
relpath -  - Set whether the child file paths within this  are relative to the  itself or the , or inherit from parent.

 inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.

parameters.name: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.label: Use Parent's Behavior
parameters.description: inherit - Inherit setting from parent. project - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. externaltox - The path, when specified as a relative path, will be relative to the  file. When no external  file is specified, or when Enable External  is not toggled on, this doesn't have any impact.
